open_firefox_from_desktop.prerequisite: The desktop is visible (no window covering it) and you can see a Firefox icon (circular orange) somewhere among the icons on the desktop

steps.reasoning: Heuristic: Desktop icons are typically interactive. Hypothesis: Clicking the Firefox icon will launch the browser. I see the icon; I will interact with it.
steps.expected_effect: Firefox browser window appears on screen

steps.reasoning: Previous action returned [OK] and I see the browser window. Task complete.
steps.success_proof: Firefox browser window with address bar visible

navigate_to_url.description: Open a URL in the active browser window
navigate_to_url.prerequisite: A browser window is the active window — you can see its address bar

steps.reasoning: Focus the address bar — Ctrl+L is a stable heuristic for mainstream browsers.
steps.expected_effect: Address bar is focused and ready for input

steps.reasoning: Type the destination URL directly to bypass UI navigation.
steps.expected_effect: URL appears in the address bar
Browser starts loading the new page

steps.reasoning: The new page has loaded. Task complete.
steps.success_proof: The requested URL or page content is visible

google_search.description: Run a Google search from the address bar
google_search.prerequisite: A browser window is the active window. Default search engine is Google.

steps.reasoning: Focus the address bar for search input.

steps.expected_effect: Search query visible in address bar

steps.reasoning: Search results are visible. Task complete.

youtube_add_comment.description: Add a comment to a YouTube video
youtube_add_comment.prerequisite: Must be on a YouTube video page, signed in

steps.reasoning: Hypothesis: Comments section is below the viewport. Scrolling to locate interaction site.

steps.target_description: the Add a comment text field
steps.expected_effect: Comment field is focused and expanded
Text appears in the comment field
Comment is submitted and visible in the thread

steps.reasoning: Comment appears in the list. Task complete.
steps.success_proof: The new comment text is visible in the comment thread

single_click_done_pattern.description: When the task is 'click the X' (singular), one successful click completes the task.
single_click_done_pattern.prerequisite: Task wording asks for a single action.

steps.target_description: the target named in the task
steps.reasoning: Locate target and click once. The servo and DPC verify the hardware action and visual change.
steps.expected_effect: The element reacts or context changes

steps.reasoning: Prior action [OK]. Task was a single click; it is now complete. Do NOT repeat successful actions.

prior_ok_means_advance_not_repeat.description: A successful prior step ([OK] in history) means you MUST move forward or finish.
prior_ok_means_advance_not_repeat.prerequisite: Action history shows the previous step ended in [OK].

steps.reasoning: Previous action returned [OK]. The system has verified the action's physical and visual effect. Do not interpret the task header in the prompt as a request to redo work. If no further visible change is required, the task is complete.
steps.success_proof: Action was verified and goal is met
